text.title: procurement-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for procurement-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Lowest-price wins: selecting the cheapest vendor without weighting quality, experience, and risk.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Price is the most legible criterion but the weakest predictor of delivery success. Low-price vendors frequently compensate by minimising scope or requesting change orders.

failure-mode.description: Acceptance criteria written as subjective goals ("high quality code", "good performance").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Subjective criteria cannot be evaluated at acceptance time, creating disputes. Require testable thresholds: defect density, response time, test coverage percentage.

failure-mode.description: Evaluation matrix weighted at 5–10% for references, then not interviewing references in a structured way.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: References are positive-bias by selection; without a rubric the criterion adds no information and can be gamed.

failure-mode.description: Multi-supplier / prime + sub arrangements treated as a single-vendor procurement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Partner ecosystems require separate SOWs, liability chains, and IP ownership clauses per party. Combining them obscures accountability.

failure-mode.description: Legal clauses are jurisdiction-specific; LLMs default to US-style clauses and miss EU GDPR processor terms, UK MAC clauses, German UWG provisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require legal review for cross-border SOWs; do not rely on agent-drafted clauses.

failure-mode.description: Indemnification caps drift: agents draft "uncapped" indemnification by default — require explicit cap (often 1x or 2x annual fees).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Review every generated contract clause for liability caps before sharing with vendors.

failure-mode.description: Data residency / sub-processor language is missing from generic LLM SOW templates; mandatory for GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2 buyers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If compliance is required, specify it in the agent prompt explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Currency drift in multi-region SOWs: agents put a USD figure in a EU SOW without an exchange clause; demand price-adjustment language.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock exchange rates or add an explicit price-adjustment clause for multi-currency SOWs.

failure-mode.description: Make-vs-buy bias: agents tend to recommend "buy" for software (training data favours SaaS marketing); explicitly weight switching cost and IP ownership.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide explicit guidance on make-vs-buy weighting before agent analysis.

failure-mode.description: Vendor-name hallucination is common in shortlist drafting; always require a user-supplied vendor list, never let the agent invent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Supply a curated vendor list as agent input; never let it generate candidate names.

failure-mode.description: Evaluation matrix can be reverse-engineered: if you tell an LLM the answer, it will weight the criteria to justify it. Lock weights before scoring.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Define and commit to evaluation weights before receiving proposals.

failure-mode.description: Acceptance criteria written by agents are often subjective ("high quality"); demand testable criteria (% test coverage, defect density, response time).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Review every acceptance criterion for objectivity and testability before signing SOW.
